reviews.author: Arkadiusz
reviews.text: Mỹ Sơn is a place that is absolutely worth visiting — one of the most fascinating archaeological sites in Vietnam and a true journey back in time. The ancient Cham temple complex, hidden in a lush green valley, makes a strong impression with its atmosphere, history, and the surrounding nature. 🏛️ Incredible history and architecture Walking among centuries‑old temples is an unforgettable experience. Although part of the complex was destroyed, the remaining structures still impress with their details and unique character. Here you can truly feel the rich heritage and architectural mastery of the Champa civilization. 🌿 Beautiful natural surroundings Mỹ Sơn enchants not only with its ruins but also with the scenery. The mist-covered hills, the sounds of nature, and the peaceful valley create a magical atmosphere unlike anywhere else. It’s the perfect place to slow down, connect with nature, and take a break from the hustle and bustle of the city. 🧭 Well prepared for visitors The paths are well marked, the area is maintained, and informational signs help visitors understand the history of each temple group. The staff are friendly and helpful, contributing to a calm and pleasant experience throughout the visit. ⭐ Summary Mỹ Sơn is a must‑see for anyone interested in history, culture, or simply looking for a unique and memorable excursion. Its magical atmosphere, beautiful setting, and impressive ruins make it a place that stays with you long after the visit. Highly recommended!

reviews.author: Kirk MacDonald
reviews.text: My Son Sanctuary is a UNESCO World Heritage site in central Vietnam (Quang Nam Province) featuring 4th–13th century Hindu tower temples built by the Champa Kingdom. Located near Hoi An, these brick ruins dedicated to Shiva are a major cultural site. Loved learning about the Champa civilization and sad to learn that the site was heavily damaged by bombing during the Vietnam War. Best Time to Visit: Early morning (6:00–9:00 AM) to avoid heat and crowds, or late afternoon for better light.

reviews.author: Sin Weng
reviews.text: This place was awesome. All the little towns leading up to this place on my ride there was really cool to drive through. I paid 150k and parking on site was 5k. You walk a bit and an electric cart car will take you to the entrance, then you walk some more. It is a decent amount of walking. I would imagine it gets a little uncomfortable during the hottest parts of the day. Some of the sites are originals and some are being rebuilt so just keep that in mind. Pro tip: the trick to avoid crowds is to either come right when they open or right when they close. I did the latter and there were only a few tourists there. Plus the golden hour light was beautiful on the temple walls. The only thing about visiting at the end of the day is that your time is limited. They sell the last ticket at 5pm and they officially close at 5:30pm.

reviews.author: Z M
reviews.text: Really cool Hindu temples built by the Champa Kingdom in the 4th-to-13th-century. Most of them were ruined by the war in the 60's. It's in a national park surrounded by beautiful nature. It's nice because you could see the ruins and then have a really peaceful walk around the park. They also have a bunch of cafes and food in and outside the park.

reviews.author: David Farrell
reviews.text: Truly spectacular place to visit, so atmospheric and awe-inspiring. To be in the presence of something so ancient and significant is quite an experience and privilege. Yes it's busy but worth it. We booked a shuttle bus from Hoi An via Trip Advisor arriving at 0900 and leaving 1200 which was just about enough time. It was a small group, with a friendly driver.

reviews.author: RealReviews
reviews.text: I had a great time seeing that UNESCO site. You start by buying a entrance ticket for 150k VND, included in that ticket is a ride towards the site and back. You could also walk that distance which is roughly 2km. Once you reached My Son, you see this very old remarks from a different timeline. You can just imagine how massive that must have been. You can also rent a bicycle for 30k VND so you don’t have to walk around. I enjoyed it to walk tho since it was such a relaxing atmosphere. Great place!

reviews.author: Chiang Paul
reviews.text: After parking your car, you can use the restroom. Then you need to pay for a ticket to enter the main gate. Walk 300 meters forward and take the shuttle bus to the scenic area. There is a traditional performance at 10:00 AM. You can also see historical sites and ruins. The round trip takes about 2 hours.

reviews.author: J M
reviews.text: Incredible archeological sights that if you let it really takes you back in time. Do not miss, it takes a little time to get here from the city but the ride is beautiful and the historical sight is so worth it. It is situated in such a beautiful location!

reviews.author: Oleg Valyn
reviews.text: This place was surprising. First of all, I want to say that I’m not a big fan of tourist spots packed with huge crowds of people, with that chaotic, overwhelming energy. Here everything is balanced. It smells very nice, the atmosphere is wonderful and uplifting, and there are very few people. Even the temple structures themselves are genuinely interesting to explore. Moreover, unlike typical tourist locations, here you can touch almost everything. There are no barriers or restrictions, and everything relies on your own good sense, which is a big advantage. In total, you’ll need around 30–40 minutes for the whole complex. But if you want to spend more time here, maybe even meditate, it’s a beautiful place where you can easily spend a couple of hours. Recommended.

reviews.author: Raymond rodrigues
reviews.text: My Son is a marvellous ancient ruins. It used to be Lord Siva temple. It’s a UNESCO world heritage site. It’s a good place to know the history for the country. I had visited the site on October 2025, at that time a couple of sites were under restoration process. Buggy service available for free to drop and pick you up from the main gate to the inside restaurant. From there you will be on your own to explore the site by walk. You don’t need any guide as there is an App to do the job. There will be QR codes at the site, you scan the code through the app and explore with audio guide. It’s a good place for photography also. It will take approx 3 hours to explore the site. Good time to visit in a day is morning earliest possible. So plan your trip accordingly. Have a good trip.

reviews.author: 陳俊傑
reviews.text: My Son Sanctuary, a Hindu holy site and Cham ruins – architecture and ancient civilization. This is a group of partially destroyed temples, once thriving religious sites for the early Cham people, but long abandoned. This holy site is a UNESCO World Heritage Site, featuring stone carvings of Hindu deities and towers nestled in the tropical jungle.

reviews.author: Liam Doherty
reviews.text: Disclaimer: I didn’t use a guide or the audio guide. Mỹ Sơn is an interesting site if you’re into history and have some basic understanding of Hindu culture. The complex is large enough to spend a few hours exploring, and you don’t really need to hire a bike — I walked the whole site comfortably. I enjoyed the visit more because I researched the area as I went, which helped me understand the significance of the different buildings and objects. Without that context, a lot of the site could feel underwhelming. I was told the audio guide isn’t very good, as it focuses more on dimensions and measurements rather than historical or cultural context. Some parts of the site are reconstructed rather than original, which you can notice by differences in brick age and construction. The original temples used very specific brick-bonding techniques that stand out once you know what to look for. Overall, it’s a pleasant and interesting place, but unless you’re a history buff, it may not feel as impressive as some other heritage sites. I enjoyed it personally, but I’ve seen more engaging ruins elsewhere. Still worth visiting if you have time — just do a bit of research beforehand to really appreciate it.

reviews.author: Zimi Brown
reviews.text: My Son Sanctuary is absolutely worth visiting, BUT just make sure you go early. We arrived around 6:30 AM and it was calm, quiet and truly atmospheric. The sunrise was beautiful and made the experience special. Later in the morning it gets overcrowded with big tour groups, which really kills the vibe. Go early to enjoy the temples properly 🌅

reviews.author: Gaurav Agrawal
reviews.text: Ancient heritage of central Vietnam and people of Cham community This is a group of temples dedicated to various Hindu goods which were revered by locals after they influenced by Indian peoples due to trade relations. These temples are partly in ruins and conservation is going on with help of various foreign countries including India Located around 40-45 km from hoi an and can also be visited from Da Nang city The group tour is a good and affordable option to visit this place that can be booked from various online platforms The ticket for adult is priced around Rs 500 The group tour includes a guide that will be explain all the details related to this temple You can view various statues and shivlinga in this premise Morinung time is good slot to visit here The temple group is amidst forest area which makes it more senic Some of the temples were destroyed during America Vietnam war There is a cultural show scheduled around every hour that includes local folk dance. The place is very neatly and cleanly maintained You need to walk some distance from the entry gate to main temple group,may put stress on seniors

reviews.author: Christopher Mio
reviews.text: A very nice ride through the countryside and small towns led me to this beautiful place. Very well thought out layout allowed for a peaceful walk through the lush valley. Good markers explained important details about the sight, its historical significance, and importance for preservation.

reviews.author: Masha Ostroumova
reviews.text: Really impressive, like stepping into a long lost city in the jungle. I was lucky to see a monkey as an added bonus. The dancing and signing performance was a nice bonus although the trumpet sounded like my migraine soundtrack. It was great though, I would recommend to anyone visiting Hoi An to add this place to the itinerary.

reviews.author: Emma Betts
reviews.text: My husband and I were traveling for our honeymoon and made sure to add this experience to our list. We had a guide for our tour; however, there is also a self-guided option with audio available, and we saw many solo travelers enjoying the site as well. Before the buggy ride out, we were treated to a beautiful dance performance. The women danced with holy water bowls balanced gracefully on their heads, sharing the story of the Cham people and the reverence they held for their gods. It was a rainy day for us, but the mist, quiet air, and softened stone only deepened the atmosphere and made the experience feel even more meaningful. The temple structures themselves are absolutely breathtaking. Built centuries ago using bricks bonded with sugarcane resin, their craftsmanship remains remarkably intact despite time, weather, and war. Standing among them feels humbling, as if the stones are still holding the devotion, rituals, and history of those who built them. This is truly a must-see, must-feel experience.

reviews.author: Vasileios Vasilakis
reviews.text: Go there early! Before the tiursitd show up! We enjoyed it and we had a very good guide that gave us lots of information. Go early, enjoy the temples, go to the show (I think it's at 9:45) and then take some last pics and leave. It's very convenient that way and less crowded.

reviews.author: KenJi Mr
reviews.text: My Son Sanctuary is truly a must-see destination for anyone interested in history and culture. The ancient Hindu temples, nestled in a lush green valley, create a peaceful and mystical atmosphere that is deeply moving. We were amazed by the intricate brickwork and the enduring spirit of the Champa ruins, which have stood the test of time for centuries. ​Walking through the site felt like stepping into another era. The surrounding mountains and the quiet beauty of the landscape made the experience very special. It is a place of great historical significance, and seeing the preservation efforts firsthand was impressive. Whether you are a history buff or just looking for a serene escape into nature, My Son offers a unique and unforgettable experience. It was definitely one of the most meaningful parts of our journey.

reviews.author: Expeditionsolo Luke
reviews.text: This place was beautiful!! So happy I went here today. So much history here. Enjoyed walking around the complex it's big. Takes a few hours to see everything here. Definitely worth coming here. Quiet, peaceful and relaxing. I loved it and recommend this place to everyone. ❤️🇻🇳🙏

reviews.author: Hunter Reed
reviews.text: We did a guided tour of My Son. We went in the morning (pickup 7:30am at hotel) and the site was very crowded with other tour groups. Hard to get photos due to the amount of people, but totally worth visiting to learn about the history of the Champ people and the valley.

reviews.author: Dane May
reviews.text: Very cool visit. Similar vibe to Ankor Wat as in you can walk around freely without barriers and explore at your own pace. Staff drive electric buggies and transport you from entrance to main archaeological site free of charge. It can get busy during lunchtime. Tickets online or at the gate. No ques.

reviews.author: Annielovenature
reviews.text: A historic site. The majority were ruined during the war, it is a cooperation project between Vietnam and India to rebuild. Beyond the history part, it is also closely related to the culture and the religion. For Vietnamese citizens, the ticket price is 100.000vnd. For non-Vietnamese citizens, the ticket price is 150.000vnd. It’s located at the mountains, rivers. Nice place to far away from the city They have the shows as well. Don’t miss it out. It lasts for about 20 minutes

reviews.author: Steve Cartlidge
reviews.text: Lovely quiet place to cycle to. Get there early to beat the 'normal' tourists! And book yourself a room at 'My Son Heritage Resort and Spa' - Just BEAUTIFUL 🤗 🚲 🚲

reviews.author: Gilles Prigent
reviews.text: Nice historic site made of ancient ruined Hindu temples easily accessible from Danang and Hoi An. We visited in low rainy season and were the only visitors on site which was quite spectacular in many ways ! If you feel like walking a bit, it is only a 2-km walk from the entrance to the site and quite a lovely journey. There is a 15-minute traditional show at the end of the tour to close the visit. First show of the day this time of year was at 9:45am. Therefore all tourist buses and groups are not aiming to arrive on site before 8:30-9:00. I did not feel it was so impressive but definitely a nice and interesting visit.

reviews.author: Graham Childers
reviews.text: This is a place you go to and just feel like you’ve stumbled apon a lost jungle world. We went close to sunset which gave it this movie like atmosphere. It was overcast and just the perfect vibe to experience these ruins. Go see it if you are in the area and like ruins i loved it!!

reviews.author: Tegan Mordin
reviews.text: Easy to get a grab from Hoi An center, adviced to get a tour but my partner and I didn’t and still learnt a lot from the information boards which were filled with history! An amazing space, some nice places to eat before entering. Respectful to have your shoulders and legs covered as in any sacred space.

reviews.author: Michael Schaffer
reviews.text: We visited very early today (~7AM) and we were the first guests to enter the area which was very nice. Overall we had a nice morning there and the temples, altars and other Chamba buildings were very interesting. Can highly recommend to do the tour with a guide.

reviews.author: Jeff Slater
reviews.text: 1000+ year old temple that was very interesting. Preservation is underway at the site and you can see how they are uncovering more buildings and expanding the opportunities to see more. Tour from Hoi An was good with lunch and a short boat ride

reviews.author: Mahgol Motalebi
reviews.text: Rather a long journey from Danang. Nice to see but did nit impress me enough! Motorbike park almost at the end of the road near entrance in the right side. Entrance is 150k. There are electric buggies to take you closer. Visit take around 1 hour.
